title: hexa-lang stdlib + gate domain — first ω-cycle witness

trigger.rationale: META_ROI deferred until paper 100% draft; next autonomous-safe axis

axes.claim: let spawn="x" parses as Spawn keyword stmt → variable collapses to void at runtime; `hexa run` exits 0 (silent failure).
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let spawn="x"; println(to_string(spawn))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1

axes.claim: len("café") == 5 (UTF-8 byte count of 4 chars), NOT 4 — len returns bytes on string.
arr.push(x) BOTH mutates arr in-place AND returns the new array — STDLIB_MAP claims pure-return; in-place mutation also visible.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let mut a=[1,2]; let r=a.push(3); println(to_string(len(a))); println(to_string(r))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1

axes.claim: exec() invokes /bin/sh (POSIX), NOT user's $SHELL — bashisms and zsh syntax unavailable.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){println(exec("echo $0"))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1

axes.claim: file_exists() follows symlinks — dangling symlink returns false despite the link itself existing.
axes.cmd: ln -sf /tmp/__nope__ /tmp/__sl_test__; echo 'fn main(){println(to_string(file_exists("/tmp/__sl_test__")))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1

axes.claim: to_int has TWO failure modes: 'abc' → stderr error + exit=0 (silent); '42abc' → stderr error + exit=1 (hard). STDLIB_MAP §2 'throws — wrap in try' is half-true.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let n=to_int("abc"); println("survived")}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1; echo exit=$?

axes.claim: sys_args identifier unbound by default — evaluates to silent void; CLI args reachable only via HEXA_ARGV env workaround.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){println(to_string(sys_args))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin one two 2>&1 | head -1

axes.claim: exec("false") returns empty string AND hexa exits 0 — child non-zero exit is silently dropped; no exitcode access from exec() return alone.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let r=exec("false"); println("after="+to_string(len(r)))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1; echo exit=$?

axes.claim: s[0] on string DOES work — returns 1-char string, contradicting STDLIB_MAP §2 'string indexing is not direct'.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let s="abc"; println(to_string(len(s[0])))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1

axes.claim: `let x=1; x=2;` reassignment works WITHOUT `mut` — runtime treats mut as advisory; immutability NOT enforced.
axes.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let x=1; x=2; println(to_string(x))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1
axes.doc: HEXA_RESERVED.md §1a lists mut reserved; runtime doesn't enforce

falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.claim: let spawn=...; → void+exit0 (silent-void hazard, gate/ author bug-class)
falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let spawn="x"; println(to_string(spawn))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1
echo 'fn main(){let mut a=[1,2]; let r=a.push(3); println(to_string(len(a))); println(to_string(r))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1
echo 'fn main(){println(exec("echo $0"))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1

falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.claim: file_exists() follows symlinks — dangling symlink → false
falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.cmd: ln -sf /tmp/__nope_F137__ /tmp/__sl_F137__; echo 'fn main(){println(to_string(file_exists("/tmp/__sl_F137__")))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1 | head -1

falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.claim: to_int("abc") → exit=0 silent; to_int("42abc") → exit=1 hard (bifurcated)
falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.cmd: echo 'fn main(){let n=to_int("abc"); println("survived")}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin 2>&1; echo exit=$?

falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.claim: sys_args unbound → silent void; argv requires HEXA_ARGV workaround
falsifier_candidates_F133_F139.cmd: echo 'fn main(){println(to_string(sys_args))}' | HEXA_RESOLVER_NO_REROUTE=1 hexa run /dev/stdin one two 2>&1 | head -1

main_thread_action_required: user-go for atlas merge + falsifier promote — F133-F139 staged here as raw71 SUGGEST mode (no auto-write to nexus/state/falsifier_candidates.jsonl). On user-go: append 7 records, recompute falsifier_registry.sha256, refresh hive index.

cold_start_cost_estimate.actual_includes: repo inventory + 10 empirical hexa runtime probes + sha256 batch + witness write
cold_start_cost_estimate.interpretation: scout 45x conservative — hexa-lang has unusually pre-curated invariant docs (HEXA_RESERVED.md + STDLIB_MAP.md) which front-loaded discovery; falsifiable claims already enumerated as 'pitfalls'. Future axes with similar pre-curated pitfall docs can use 5-10 min budget.

ROI_assessment.rationale: 10 anchors verified single session, 8 promote-worthy Tier-1, 2 contradict existing docs (downgraded to Tier-2 doc-drift observations). silent-void family (spawn, sys_args, to_int-pure-garbage, exec-false) reveals coherent 'silent failure' bug-class spanning 4 subsystems — domain has internal coherence beyond surface enumeration.
ROI_assessment.axis_density_extrapolated: ~440/h equivalent surface; honest cap ~30-50 before diminishing returns
ROI_assessment.raw_73_admissibility: no overhype — 8 verified Tier-1, 2 explicit doc-contradictions downgraded

hexa_sim.potential: shared falsifier — both depend on /bin/sh semantics
cross-bridge witness — hexa-sim atlas may have false 'success' from this swallowing

design_strategy.potential: violates 'reason+fix trailer' since exit=0 on error; feeds raw 66 enforce-layer-secondary backlog

next_actions: user-go: review 7 F-candidates (F133-F139) and approve atlas append
